@@ -0,0 +1,103 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: d080
+title: Fix "New" so it works uniformly on every grid — prove via logs, fix stale view YAMLs, surface silent field-name drops
+---
+## What
+
+User reports that "New" works on the **tag** grid but fails on the **task** grid and **project** grid. Log evidence over the last 2-3h:
+
+- `entity.add:tag` dispatched + succeeded → wrote `.kanban/tags/01KPCRBX9ZBNVH3WZVVCSQ7HH6.{yaml,jsonl}` at 20:39:46.
+- `entity.add:task` — **zero** dispatches hit the backend.
+- `entity.add:project` — **zero** dispatches hit the backend.
+- Only a legacy `task.add` from scope `[window:main, engine]` was attempted + rejected with "command not available in current context" (that's the old board-view path, tracked separately).
+
+All three grids render the same `AddEntityBar` component (kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx:536-561) with the same click handler `() => addNewEntity(dispatch, entityType)` → `dispatch("entity.add:${entityType}")`. `AddEntity::build_and_write` has unit tests that prove it works for all three entity types. So **the backend and the click handler are fine in isolation**. What's failing is *everything between the click and the dispatch reaching Rust* — or the user is perceiving "doesn't work" because of stale view YAMLs making the grids render empty/broken and look like the button is missing.
+
+Either way, **"New" must work end-to-end on all three grids identically**. This card closes that.
+
+## Approach
+
+### 1. Fix the stale view YAMLs (blocks the user from seeing the grids at all)
+
+The view YAMLs declare `card_fields` that reference non-existent entity fields. `grid-view.tsx:184-187` does `fieldNames.map(name => fieldMap.get(name)).filter(f => f !== undefined)` — unknown names are **silently dropped**. No error, no warning.
+
+- **`.kanban/views/01JMVIEW0000000000TGRID0.yaml` ("Tasks Grid")** — `card_fields: [title, status, tags, assignees, priority, due_date]`. Task entity (`builtin/entities/task.yaml`) has `title`, `tags`, `assignees`, `due` — no `status`, no `priority`, and the field is `due` not `due_date`. Three of six drop silently. Update `card_fields` to reference real task fields only (e.g. `[title, tags, assignees, due, position_column]` — pick what actually exists). Use the entity YAML as the source of truth for field names.
+- **`.kanban/views/01JMVIEW0000000000PGRID0.yaml` ("Projects")** — `card_fields: [name, description, color, order]`. Project entity has `name, description, color` — no `order`. Drop `order`.
+
+### 2. Surface the silent drop in `grid-view.tsx`
+
+- **`kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx:184-187`** — when a `card_fields` entry does not resolve to a known `FieldDef`, emit a single `console.warn` per unknown field per view (not per render) naming the view id and the bad field name. Use `console.warn` per the `frontend-logging` memory note — it's what shows up in `log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"'`.
+- The warn must include: view id, view name, bad field name, list of valid field names for the entity. So a future author sees the typo immediately.
+
+### 3. Verify the + button dispatch fires on all grids — log-driven proof
+
+This is the actual "New isn't working" part. Run the app, navigate to each grid, click the + button, watch the log.
+
+- **Terminal 1 (log stream)**: `log stream --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"' --style compact`
+- **Terminal 2**: `cd kanban-app && cargo tauri dev`
+
+For each of Tag, Task, Project grid:
+1. Navigate to the grid via the left-nav.
+2. Click the + button below the table.
+3. Confirm in the log stream that `command cmd=entity.add:{type} ...` appears AND that the backend writes a new file to `.kanban/{tags,tasks,projects}/`.
+4. Confirm the new entity appears as a new row in the grid (after stale YAMLs are fixed).
+
+If any of the three grids shows NO log line for `entity.add:{type}` after clicking +, that means the click handler is not firing. Instrument the `onClick` in `AddEntityBar` with `console.warn` (not `console.log` per logging memory) to prove where it stops. Likely culprits to check if dispatch doesn't fire:
+- `data.entityType === ""` guard at `grid-view.tsx` accidentally true (should not be — all three views have valid `entity_type`).
+- `useDispatchCommand` resolving to a frontend `execute` handler that no-ops (`command-scope.tsx:446-449`). `resolveCommand(effectiveScope, "entity.add:task")` should return `null` so it falls through to backend dispatch. Verify by logging `resolved` in that path.
+- A parent `CommandScopeProvider` somehow intercepting or overriding the dispatch path.
+
+### 4. Same flow for the context menu + command palette
+
+- Right-click inside each grid body. "New Task" / "New Tag" / "New Project" must appear. Click it. Confirm dispatch in logs.
+- `Cmd+K` → command palette → type "new". "New Task" / "New Tag" / "New Project" must appear depending on the active view. Select, confirm dispatch.
+
+### 5. Test coverage — lock in uniform behavior
+
+- **`kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.test.tsx`** — extend the existing `RunCommandProbe` pattern. Add a parameterised test that for each of `task`, `tag`, `project`:
+  - Renders `GridView` with a view declaring that `entity_type`.
+  - Clicks the AddEntityBar button.
+  - Asserts `mockInvoke` received `{cmd: "entity.add:{type}", ...}`.
+  - Additionally: asserts the `grid.newBelow` keyboard command dispatches the same.
+- **`swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_dispatch_integration.rs`** — add `dispatch_entity_add_project_creates_project_with_defaults` mirroring `dispatch_entity_add_tag_creates_tag_with_defaults`. Asserts `.kanban/projects/` gains a file with the `name` schema default.
+- **New test file**: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.stale-card-fields.test.tsx` — assert `console.warn` is called once per unknown field when the view YAML's `card_fields` references an invalid name.
+
+## Files to modify
+
+- `.kanban/views/01JMVIEW0000000000TGRID0.yaml` — tasks grid, fix `card_fields`.
+- `.kanban/views/01JMVIEW0000000000PGRID0.yaml` — projects grid, fix `card_fields`.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` — surface unknown-field warnings; (maybe) add tracing on the click handler.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.test.tsx` — parameterised dispatch-fires test across task/tag/project.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_dispatch_integration.rs` — end-to-end project test.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Clicking `+` on the tasks grid dispatches `entity.add:task` and creates a task (verified in `log stream`).
+- [ ] Clicking `+` on the projects grid dispatches `entity.add:project` and creates a project (verified in `log stream`).
+- [ ] Clicking `+` on the tags grid still dispatches `entity.add:tag` and creates a tag.
+- [ ] Right-click inside each grid shows "New {Type}" and it fires.
+- [ ] Cmd+K palette shows "New {Type}" for the active grid's entity type and it fires.
+- [ ] After creating an entity on each grid, a new visible row appears (requires stale `card_fields` fix).
+- [ ] `grep -r "console.warn.*unknown card_field" kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` returns a single matching warn call site.
+- [ ] Loading a grid whose view YAML references a non-existent field logs a warn naming the view + bad field + valid fields list.
+- [ ] Unit test in `grid-view.test.tsx` asserts the + button dispatches `entity.add:{type}` for each of task, tag, project (parameterised).
+- [ ] `command_dispatch_integration.rs` has a new test proving `entity.add:project` creates a file in `.kanban/projects/`.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Parameterised grid-view test: task, tag, project — + button dispatches `entity.add:{type}`.
+- [ ] Parameterised grid-view test: `grid.newBelow` / `grid.newAbove` dispatch `entity.add:{type}` for each entity type.
+- [ ] Backend: `dispatch_entity_add_project_creates_project_with_defaults` — mirror the tag test.
+- [ ] Frontend: warn-on-unknown-card_field test.
+- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run src/components/grid-view` — all pass.
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban --test command_dispatch_integration` — all pass.
+- [ ] Manual verification: live-run kanban-app, click + on each grid, watch `log stream` prove each entity created.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first (start with the parameterised grid-view test and the project integration test), then fix the stale YAMLs and the silent-drop warning to make them pass.
+
+#entity
\ No newline at end of file

## What

User reports that "New" works on the **tag** grid but fails on the **task** grid and **project** grid. Log evidence over the last 2-3h:

- `entity.add:tag` dispatched + succeeded → wrote `.kanban/tags/01KPCRBX9ZBNVH3WZVVCSQ7HH6.{yaml,jsonl}` at 20:39:46.
- `entity.add:task` — **zero** dispatches hit the backend.
- `entity.add:project` — **zero** dispatches hit the backend.
- Only a legacy `task.add` from scope `[window:main, engine]` was attempted + rejected with "command not available in current context" (that's the old board-view path, tracked separately).

What's failing is *everything between the click and the dispatch reaching Rust* — or the user is perceiving "doesn't work" because of stale view YAMLs making the grids render empty/broken and look like the button is missing.

Either way, **"New" must work end-to-end on all three grids identically**. This card closes that.

## Approach

### 1. Fix the stale view YAMLs (blocks the user from seeing the grids at all)

The view YAMLs declare `card_fields` that reference non-existent entity fields. `grid-view.tsx:184-187` does `fieldNames.map(name => fieldMap.get(name)).filter(f => f !== undefined)` — unknown names are **silently dropped**. No error, no warning.

- **`.kanban/views/01JMVIEW0000000000TGRID0.yaml` ("Tasks Grid")** — `card_fields: [title, status, tags, assignees, priority, due_date]`. Task entity (`builtin/entities/task.yaml`) has `title`, `tags`, `assignees`, `due` — no `status`, no `priority`, and the field is `due` not `due_date`. Three of six drop silently. Update `card_fields` to reference real task fields only (e.g. `[title, tags, assignees, due, position_column]` — pick what actually exists). Use the entity YAML as the source of truth for field names.
- **`.kanban/views/01JMVIEW0000000000PGRID0.yaml` ("Projects")** — `card_fields: [name, description, color, order]`. Project entity has `name, description, color` — no `order`. Drop `order`.

### 2. Surface the silent drop in `grid-view.tsx`

- **`kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx:184-187`** — when a `card_fields` entry does not resolve to a known `FieldDef`, emit a single `console.warn` per unknown field per view (not per render) naming the view id and the bad field name. Use `console.warn` per the `frontend-logging` memory note — it's what shows up in `log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"'`.
- The warn must include: view id, view name, bad field name, list of valid field names for the entity. So a future author sees the typo immediately.

### 3. Verify the + button dispatch fires on all grids — log-driven proof

This is the actual "New isn't working" part. Run the app, navigate to each grid, click the + button, watch the log.

- **Terminal 1 (log stream)**: `log stream --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"' --style compact`
- **Terminal 2**: `cd kanban-app && cargo tauri dev`

For each of Tag, Task, Project grid:
1. Navigate to the grid via the left-nav.
2. Click the + button below the table.
3. Confirm in the log stream that `command cmd=entity.add:{type} ...` appears AND that the backend writes a new file to `.kanban/{tags,tasks,projects}/`.
4. Confirm the new entity appears as a new row in the grid (after stale YAMLs are fixed).

If any of the three grids shows NO log line for `entity.add:{type}` after clicking +, that means the click handler is not firing. Instrument the `onClick` in `AddEntityBar` with `console.warn` (not `console.log` per logging memory) to prove where it stops. Likely culprits to check if dispatch doesn't fire:

- Right-click inside each grid body. "New Task" / "New Tag" / "New Project" must appear. Click it. Confirm dispatch in logs.
- `Cmd+K` → command palette → type "new". "New Task" / "New Tag" / "New Project" must appear depending on the active view. Select, confirm dispatch.

- **`kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.test.tsx`** — extend the existing `RunCommandProbe` pattern. Add a parameterised test that for each of `task`, `tag`, `project`:

- `.kanban/views/01JMVIEW0000000000PGRID0.yaml` — projects grid, fix `card_fields`.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` — surface unknown-field warnings; (maybe) add tracing on the click handler.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.test.tsx` — parameterised dispatch-fires test across task/tag/project.

- [ ] Clicking `+` on the projects grid dispatches `entity.add:project` and creates a project (verified in `log stream`).

- [ ] Cmd+K palette shows "New {Type}" for the active grid's entity type and it fires.
- [ ] After creating an entity on each grid, a new visible row appears (requires stale `card_fields` fix).
- [ ] `grep -r "console.warn.*unknown card_field" kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` returns a single matching warn call site.
- [ ] Loading a grid whose view YAML references a non-existent field logs a warn naming the view + bad field + valid fields list.
- [ ] Unit test in `grid-view.test.tsx` asserts the + button dispatches `entity.add:{type}` for each of task, tag, project (parameterised).
- [ ] `command_dispatch_integration.rs` has a new test proving `entity.add:project` creates a file in `.kanban/projects/`.

+## Diagnosis (2026-04-17)

-`SetActiveViewCmd` (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/ui_commands.rs`) changed `active_view` in UIState but did NOT update the `scope_chain`. When a user switched from the Board to the Tags/Projects grid, the backend's `scope_chain` still carried `view:01JMVIEW0000000000BOARD0` from the last `ui.setFocus` that ran while on the board. The command palette reads `scope_chain` from UIState → `list_commands_for_scope` saw the OLD view → `emit_entity_add` emitted `entity.add:task` (not `entity.add:tag` or `entity.add:project`) → user saw only "New Task" regardless of what grid they were on.
+The **backend emission is correct in isolation**: `emit_entity_add` (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs`) iterates the scope chain, looks up each `view:{id}`, and emits `entity.add:{type}`. `gather_views` (`kanban-app/src/commands.rs`) propagates `entity_type`. The builtin YAMLs declare `entity_type` correctly.

-All of the backend unit tests passed because they constructed the scope chain directly with `view:{tags_grid.id}` and didn't exercise the view-switch → scope-chain path.
+**Updated diagnosis (implementer)**: Every isolated backend test with the REAL builtin view registry passes — `entity_add_task_emitted_for_tasks_grid_view_scope`, `entity_add_tag_emitted_for_tags_grid_view_scope`, `entity_add_project_emitted_for_projects_grid_view_scope`, and the cross-cutting guard `list_commands_for_scope_emits_entity_add_for_every_grid_view`. The Rust emission chain is not the failure point when called with the scope chain the palette is supposed to send.

The palette reads `useUIState().scope_chain` — if that never gets a `view:*` moniker pushed into it, `emit_entity_add` has nothing to match on. `emit_entity_add` now logs each `scope_moniker` / `view_id` decision at `debug` level.

-`SetActiveViewCmd::execute` now reads the current `scope_chain`, rewrites every `view:*` moniker to point at the new view id, and writes the chain back to UIState. When the user next focuses anything in the new view, `ui.setFocus` will rebuild the full chain from scratch; this bridge keeps the palette/context-menu working in the interim (i.e. when the user hits Cmd+K immediately after switching views).
